reviews.review_description: Challenging perceptual  I get  crazy adrenaline trying to step around, duck, throw the whip and  find the gems.  Genius way to use VR.    I hope they make a sequel.  I found this very enjoyable and submersive to play.

reviews.review_title: Wow, real movement is so immersive
reviews.review_description: I wish more games would do real movement within your 2x2m sq space (I appreciate that not everyone has this space though). I remember being blown away by a demo of God for Tea, which actually uses available space so you can actually walk around for real. Though this only does it with 2x2m sq, it does an incredible job with this limited space (once I fixed a problem with Meta/App? that kept changing my border to stationary). I really fell for the illusion that I was using my own real-world steps to traverse the temple. Also, when you see all the mini-train tracks you get the promise of rides all around the temple, which definitely does not disappoint. It only takes 2-3 hours to complete, but I thought it was well worth it for the experience.

Love this game it's perfect for 
vr. You do need 2 square meters but if you can find it you'll not regret it. A definite 5 star.

Is this game really a 5/5? No, should you still buy it? YES. 

Here’s why. The 1-person dev team engineered a completely unique way to have people move around in VR that gives you the illusion of exploring a massive continuous temple complex while keeping you in your living room. It’s fun, it’s  jaw dropping, and it is the right amount of challenging.

It’s not a triple AAA story, the voice acting is a tad cheesy, and a couple textures look a little pixelated (as of 2025). But this is still an absolute masterpiece of VR game design that should be studied and copied.

I have played many adventure puzzle games and this one is by far the most fun. It takes a while (not too long) to get used to stepping from one moving block to another without losing your balance. Once you find your center of gravity it is a blast. I stayed low in my stance for a while. You actually get a bit of a workout in this game. I have played it a few times and I am playing it again. I wish there was an eye of the temple 2. Would buy it immediately. Keep up the great work. Thank you for this gem.💎

Just bought and didn´t play. I´m already disapointed because it´s not as most other apps in this store. It doesn´t come with the PCVR version, unlike most of all games. The joke is, when you use the searchbar in Link-PC, this games shows up. I wouldn´t bought it if it wouldn´t suggest that PCVR is included. I have a powerful PC and bought it over meta to get both (standalone + pcvr) like with every other game I bought from meta. I thought it is a must do for developpers to the TOC of meta to bring additional Crossbuy if there is a PCVR version that exists.

reviews.review_title: Great VR game, well worth the cost.
reviews.review_description:
I have really enjoyed the adventure, challenging in parts but not too difficult. You do need a full 2m x 2m square to play this, i did accidentally hit my tv playing this as my area was just under this size. (the TV did survive thankfully, ha ha!)
Can be a little disorientating at first when walking on a rolling stone but stick with it and you soon get used to it. 😊 👍

This is what virtual reality games should be about. Dodging obstacle courses, using every single movement to move around places without using your joystick. Adventure and solving puzzles. Let's all agree this is by far the best VR game experience ever! To the Developers please make more roomscale games like this!

This certainly was worth the money, taking us seniors awhile to finish the game. It took a short time to become comfortable with the queasy movement issues, but we thought the puzzles, graphics and music kept us going.

I have been playing VR games since 2016. I have put hours into beat saber, skyrim vr, HL alyx, both asgards, batman, pavlov and a many roguelites.. and never ever had a headache since the early days of rift dk2. This is the first game in 9 years that has given me an actual headache and vertigo in only 30 minutes of playtime. Here’s why..

The physical real world movement mechanic is an interesting proof-of-concept tech demo, but the start-stop movement of platforms is VERY nausea-inducing. There is a reason no other game has copied this movement style again. What’s shocking is that this has been a solved problem for many years. Most VR games use tunneling to reduce the effect of motion sickness by blacking out your peripheral vision during movement, but it is completely missing here.

It’s like they had a novel idea for movement in Vr so they built a puzzle game as a tech demo and shipped it without even trying to polish the user experience.

Please do not buy this game. I found this game on a top 25 list on a popular review website, and I will be very careful taking suggestions from them going forward.

developer_response.body:
Hi, sorry to hear you got motion sickness. Have you tried to turn on the game's various VR comfort options? Using the application menu button on the left controller, you can open the in-game menu and go to Options > VR Comfort and enable everything there. Might be worth a shot.

Very few people get motion sickness from this game, which is why it's rated high and appear on many top VR games lists, but it's a very individual thing. Despite common misconceptions, it's not very related to how many other VR games one has played (although it can sometimes influence it); people's sense of balance just work a bit differently for each individual. That's why the comfort options are there.

The floor grid and platform light cages provide a visual anchor to your physical location which can help prevent motion sickness.

Tunneling would not work for the starting and stopping of the platforms, as it would be a constant distraction and undermine the player's awareness of their surroundings. Tunneling is however used in the minecart when turning corners.

The game was developed with hundreds of people playtesting it, both VR veterans and people who had never tried any VR before, and extensive work was put into polishing the experience as much as possible. The end result works well out of the box for the large majority of people, and the comfort options are there for the rest.

I hope you will give the comfort options a try and end up having a good time with the game!

reviews.review_description:
Richie's Plank walk meets Mario Brothers meets Indiana Jones. Loved how I could walk around my living room without ever hitting a boundary wall. Great graphics, loved the whip, torch, and problem solving. Really liked walking backwards on the rolling barrels. Great game, can't wait to play more! 

I'm the dev for Solara One (Meta Quest outer space adventure)  so I can really appreciate the time that would go into creating an awesome title like this...

Genuinely, this game has created so much laughter and so much fun. However, there are a few things that I can't seem to figure out. I don't know if they're supposed to be a map overlay but I'm stuck in an area and I don't know how to get back out. But the one thing that's missing that I think is important is that you can't delete a game. You have three options but you can't just go in and delete one and start all the way over. If you can, if there's actually a way to do that I can't seem to figure it out so input accepted on that one.

developer_response.body: Hi, glad to hear the game brought you fun and laughter! You can delete save slots - crouch down in front of them to see how. If you are stuck in the game and need help or hints, please consider joining our Discord server, as it's not practical to ask for details and provide replies via a review. Find a link to the Discord server at https://eyeofthetemple.com/

reviews.review_description: The game is mostly moving from block to block to get to a switch. And then moving back through the area to get to another block. Whip can be finicky and annoying to use. Puzzles are simplistic. It’s fine, but wish there were more interesting set-pieces and different looking areas.

reviews.review_title: Best adventure game on VR -- Indy style!
reviews.review_description:
I have to confess that I have been massively in love with the PlayStation Indiana Jones games (and the "Emperor's Tomb" one in particular). This game (I am still playing it, for several evening by now) brings back the emotions and fun I remember from the early console age. 

But "Eye" is not just a nostalgic rehash. Instead, it is a quite fantastic and unique combination of environmental magic (who can resist exploring an age old temple? ;-), intellectual challenges (the intuitive and straightforward way is not always the most promising one!) which are demanding, but not over-demanding, and physical challenges which make PERFECT use of the immersive nature of virtual reality -- stepping from one stone to another, doing so in deep caves and on high plateaus which will confront all kinds of phobias that you carry around with you... -- but again, not in a way that is beyond doable, and accomplishing the tasks might actually help a little to face those phobias, as it did in my case! (And yes, there might be a reason for the mine cart track you see in several places around the temple... ;-) 

The producers also did a great job with the game's narrative and the way it is told -- mystically and non-linear (but it still all seems to 'make sense'), obviously crafted with a true love for the classical adventure stories. 

Finally, the animation is also marvelous -- not high-fidelity detailed, but absolutely fine and aesthetically appealing. The only thing that I have missed was a multiplayer feature, but my son and I kind of overcame that limitation by playing it parallel with two headsets, being connected via Meta's audio call feature. Highly recommended game for everyone who loves Indiana Jones and similar adventurous hero archetypes -- and always wanted to crack the whip in the ruins of a mystical temple! ;-)

reviews.review_title: “ WHIP IT … , WHIP IT GOOD !!! “ - DEVO.
reviews.review_description:
EYE OF THE TEMPLE IS JUST LIKE BEING IN AN INDIANA JONES MOVIE , BUT … , YOUR THE STAR !!! 

“ FELT LIKE HARRISON FORD ! “ 

“ AWESOME PHYSICS “ . 

“ FUN DOES’NT EVEN  BEGIN TO DESCRIBE IT !! “ .
This is such a unique and amazing game! Everyone must try it! I’m already hoping for a sequel 🙏
Literally the coolest game ever!!! Think temple run meets Indiana Jones meets exercise. I love that it’s a one time purchase too!!!!
This is by far the most immersive game i have played so far. Just fantastic.

Really good port of the PC version! 
I was only wondering if it's possible to have higher texture resolution for the Quest 3? I know it's not a big game, on PC 786MB.  On the Quest <300MB. But some texture resolution looks worse in comparison. Also a white glitchy pixel is visible on moving blocks.

developer_response.body:
Hi, glad you're enjoying the game!

We already squeezed in as many graphical improvements we could for the Quest 3. See our update here for details: 
https://www.oculus.com/experiences/quest/developer-post/393155290278925/

The texture resolution being lower on Quest 3 than on PC is not about file size on the hard drive; it's about rendering performance. To make the texture resolution even higher we would have had to sacrifice other aspects of the quality instead, or lower the frame rate. We chose the tradeoffs we felt provided the best experience.

Nobody else have reported a white glitchy pixel before, and we can't see it in videos people post from the game. Reviews are not a suitable channel for discussing bug reports, so if you'd like to provide us with more details about this, could you join our Discord server? See the link at https://eyeofthetemple.com/ . Cheers!

reviews.review_description:
After 3 years of VR this game still surprised me where I didn't think that would be possible anymore! Not only surprised but straight forward flashed me! Never have I felt so within a virtual world!

Every single step is done physically so you feel like you just cross this giant temple by your own feet! And although this isn't a typical platformer (you don't jump) I had Super Mario flashbacks more than once and that is a dream come true.

So I was curious if the game could hold up duringe the entire playing time and it could: new abilities every now and then, new puzzles new landscape, keeps the gameplay fresh and there are some hidden items for completionists as well.

Perfect game, this is what VR was meant to be, get it!

You'll immediately notice and be enthralled by the very unusual style of movement on offer here.  You can ONLY control your character through room scale movements as the game makes use of your real world play space with a cleverly placed web of floating blocks guiding you across the temple.  It's a pretty novel mechanic, and will keep you hooked provided you have the space (and the stomach, your VR legs WILL be tested).  But eventually the novelty wears off, and I found myself tiring of the slow paced stepping and sliding, particularly as this is an adventure puzzler and you don't always know exactly where you're supposed to be going. You could be back tracking a lot trying to find the next available path. Enjoyable, but only advisable if you have the space, stomach, and patience to see you through.

reviews.review_title: Just the best temple exploration game
reviews.review_description:
I've loved this game on PC and it's just as great on Quest. In fact, playing wireless it's even more fun and there is very little difference visually. Excellent port!

What can I say about the game itself, that hasn't been said? Moving through a vast world without any artificial movement is still dang impressive. The puzzle elements are really cool, especially those in the hidden rooms (yes, there are four cleverly hidden rooms/areas, that are whole new puzzles in themselves. You haven't truly finished this game if you haven't found and beaten them!).

Add to that some whip action and there's your Indiana Jones simulator. The only bad thing I can say about this game is that a sequel is highly unlikely. But I'm so glad it exists!

Within minutes playing the game of this EYE OF THE TEMPLE , I fell flat on the floor. Hurt both my knees and my right elbow. Potential to getting injured is high as it is such a good game and the graphic is so real  . It’s not advisable to people close to age 60 like myself coz the player must have good balance. I will ask for refund coz I only played it for few minutes with swollen knees and  hut elbow. I recommend this game to those young energetic people with good balance. You will absolutely like it like I do.

reviews.review_title: Eye of temple HELP my torch is stuck
reviews.review_description: HELP Help me please I was loving the game and my torch got stuck mid air now it's just stuck so I can't play I have tried uninstall no joy some one please HELP IM GUTTED IV INVESTED HOURS IN THE GAME HELP HELP HELP DEVS OR ANYONE!!!!

developer_response.body:
Hi, are you still having this issue? Game reviews are not a support channel and do not facilitate the kind of back and forth conversation needed to investigate an issue.

Please use either the contact email or Discord server listed at the bottom of this page to contact us for support:
https://eyeofthetemple.com/

We need more information to be able to help, as what you describe is not an issue we have heard about before.

Hope we can get this worked out.

reviews.review_description:
it looks interesting but the necessary space cleary exceeds 2x2 sqm. I have more than that but it always places the playground so that I run into walls and such. there is also no possibilty to readjust the space.
looks nice but is actually disappointing

developer_response.body: Hi! This is surprising, as many people have played it with 2m x 2m. Just checking, have you set up your Guardian boundary correctly? if you have exactly 2m x 2m, the center 9 tiles in the main menu area should be almost within the boundary, but exceed a tiny bit outside it.

reviews.review_title: Unfortunately for me it is unplayable
reviews.review_description: The game, judging by the pictures and videos, is certainly excellent, but I can't play it. This is the first VR game that I had to stop because I lost my balance on moving stones so much that I almost fell. The game warns you about this at the beginning. Unfortunately, I also seem to be sensitive to these movements.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you found the game unplayable. Did you by any chance try to enable all the options in the VR Comfort settings, such as overlay grid and light cages? These can possibly help ground you more in your play area. You can open the in-game menu using the application menu button or the B button on the Quest controllers.

reviews.review_description: I thought it was an interesting concept but got bored standing on platforms and walking on rollers after a couple of hours. Maybe you have to invest more time until it gets interesting. It needs more variety. I liked The Room and Red Matter much better. It reminded me of Jurassic World Aftermath with the walking and ducking but was much more boring than that. I’m giving up on it after a couple of hours because I don’t find it enjoyable.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you found that the game got boring. The game is somewhat simple in the beginning due to many people needing time to get familiar with the platforms, physicality, and verticality of the game. Many people have said that it gradually adds more elements to keep things fresh. The game opens up in the Creaking Gorge area - if you've completed that area and reached the lava area, you'll have a good idea of what the game can offer. If you like more action, you can also try to compete in the leaaderboards of the speedrun challenges, accessible from the main menu.

reviews.review_description: The game is very immersive and has an innovative approach for propulsion. But that's about it. The content itself is pretty boring. I am two hours in and did basically only follow what the game made me do. There is no challenge involved.

Your game doesn’t say anything about walking in real life to move in the game and not telling people that you have to do that makes me leave a bad review you’re leaving out information that some people don’t like and the point you can’t refund

developer_response.body:
The store page lists "Supported Player Modes" as only "Roomscale" which means you walk around in the game. The description text says "This game requires a play area of at least 2m x 2m." which makes it clear you will be moving around. The description also says "AN EXPERIENCE PLAYED WITH YOUR OWN BODY: Step onto blocks, flick levers, and dodge obstacles. You’ll play this physical game not with controller buttons, but your body." And lastly, the trailer shows people taking steps with their own body to get around. The store page absolutely does not leave out information but rather tries to make it clear in many different ways! We're sad to see you leave a one-star review based on claiming we're holding back information about having to walk in real life, when we've tried to make it as clear as possible for everyone.

The game is subject to Meta's normal refund policy where you can normally refund it within 14 days of the purchase if you played it for less than two hours.

reviews.review_description: Boring af

I kinda was unsure about this until I read all the reviews.
Oh mate. This is exactly what VR should be.
So we'll thought out and executed.  Really sucks you in.
Just remember the walls aren't real...you will fall over 🤣🤣😂😂

Don't hesitate to get this awesome game and watch your family and friends play. It's 😆
I wished this game is more action based rather than path finding / puzzle game.

developer_response.body: Hi! The game is indeed primarily focused on puzzles and finding your way around the temple, however, have you tried the speedrun challenges? Trying to complete small sections of the game as fast as possible (with the platform movement speed adapting to your reflexes) turns the gameplay into an action challenge.

reviews.review_description:
Stunning picturesque 
Too short however

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review!

In case haven't found the secret areas with golden idols, we would suggest you jump back into your existing save slot and give that a try using the Lens, if you're wishing the game had more to explore. It can be a challenge to find these areas but many people say it's rewarding.

reviews.review_title: Amazing beat the game found everything
reviews.review_description: Best roomscale game I’ve played so far luv watching new people get scared of the maps because it so real lol , however I’m a master gamer and need more levels or add on dlc lmk i will pay for more, awesome game / VR experience 10out of 10 , 5 stars

The space I have is barely the same as the game requires, but that has not prevented me from hitting a wall trying to avoid the dangers that lurk in the temple ... The developer has been totally successful in taking your mind to a different place. This game is incredible!!!

This game i could play for days it’s so fun if you are a Indiana Jones fan or just looking for a really fun game this is the one all the puzzles and how you actually have to move around it is  sooooo good being honest this game I played for hours and hours so if you are looking for a puzzle game or adventure game this is the best option .
I truly enjoyed playing this game, especially when with family and casting it on tv.

I certainly see what the developer was going for here. Traversing an entire temple by physically stepping on and off moving platforms, never once in the entire game will you use thumbsticks, or any of the buttons for that matter; your only means of interacting with the world around you being your torch and your whip. You will use your whip to smash pots and flip levers, and use your torch to see in the dark and to set fire to things, including occasionally yourself.
While you do move physically from platform to platform, the game is entirely designed around the fact that the real you is only in a 2m-by-2m room, so the platforms will, sometimes subtly, sometimes obviously, move you back and forth in this grid. A lot of important squares, such as all save points and whenever you need to use the whip, are arranged so you'll be in the middle of the room, so you have space to move your arms.
I thought this kind of movement would get tedious after a while, and yes, it certainly can be, but then it throws something new at you, like a new thing that wants you dead, or a new ability, or sometimes just a battle that requires you to have eyes on the back of your head to win.
The biggest downside I see to the game is that it ain't a pretty game to look at. The landscape is quite obviously voxel-based, and the textures are very muddy.
All in all, this game can be quite tedious, I certainly appreciate what the developer was going for, and I wonder if not something more could've been done with the concept. Also, there are not enough games with whips as their main weapon. 4/5.
I am thrilled with everything about this game. This is a “must have”
